description:
Goal: bring compiler2's parser output in-house by building rooted Fz-shaped quoted source values directly, so the parser front door produces the same source-root shape that jobs, macros, and compiler services consume.

Signal:
- parser/front-door APIs produce a compiler2 source root: an `AnyValueRef` into the active source heap plus the compiler-owned rooting context needed to keep it valid
- parser tests cover Elixir-shaped source forms needed by the mini-epic: function/macro definitions, modules, attributes, alias/import/require, protocol forms, quote/unquote, item calls, pipe/headless case, capture syntax, maps/structs/bitstrings, and patterns
- parser-built roots can be passed directly to source-root readers, `ir_interp` macro calls, and compiler-service forms without decoding to old `src/ast::Item` / `src/ast::Expr`
- legacy parser/AST remains legacy-owned; compiler2 does not ask the old frontend to pre-expand or desugar source

Change:
- add a compiler2 parser/front-door module that allocates/builds the quoted source values defined by `.11.7.1`
- add first-class `require` syntax or equivalent source form support at this boundary
- expose reader-facing top-level source forms and source-order metadata needed by the job-model migration in `.11.7.9`
- decide by inspection whether the existing lexer is neutral enough to reuse; fork lexer behavior too if tokenization embeds old AST policy

Why:
- quoted source production cannot be correct-by-construction if the parser first commits to an old Rust AST shape
- `require` and source-order macro availability are parser/source facts, not downstream lowering concerns
title: Compiler2 parser front door: build Fz-shaped quoted source directly
